reasoning: The 25th Amendment Section 4 has never been used in US history. Its invocation requires the Vice President and a majority of the Cabinet (who will be carefully vetted Trump loyalists) to declare the President unable to discharge his duties. A political use of Section 4 is practically impossible given his cabinet. The only realistic scenario for Section 4 invocation is a sudden, extreme medical incapacitation (like a massive stroke or coma) where Trump cannot voluntarily transfer power under Section 3, but does not die. The actuarial probability of an 78-year-old experiencing such a specific, non-fatal but completely incapacitating event within an 11-month period is exceptionally low (around 1% or less). Furthermore, the resolution criteria narrowly specify removal via Section 4, ruling out impeachment, resignation, or death. Given these extreme hurdles and the prompt's lower limit constraint, the probability is set to the floor of 0.02.
